description: Routine should produce a method evaluation with one-of-four recommendation, why-it-might-not-work-for-us section, concrete eval plan, and handoff to AI Engineer if adopting/trialing.

input_ticket.title: Method eval: should SIA adopt ColBERTv2 late-interaction retrieval?
input_ticket.description: Current SIA retrieval uses dense single-vector embeddings. Should we adopt ColBERTv2 (late interaction)? Paper claims significant recall gains. Evaluate for our setup: corpus size ~100k chunks, VPS-hosted, p95 latency SLO 500ms.

expected_output_structure: ## How it would map to our setup
## Why it might NOT work for us
smoke_questions: Is the recommendation a single-word verdict (Adopt/Trial/Hold/Reject)?
Does 'Why it might NOT work for us' honestly name constraint mismatches (not just 'we'll need to validate')?
Does the eval plan specify metric + dataset + baseline + success criteria concretely?
If recommendation is Adopt or Trial, is there a handoff to AI Engineer via a proposed type:engineering ticket?
Does the brief cite the source paper + reference implementation (not just 'there are ColBERT papers')?
success_criteria: Singular recommendation + honest why-not + full eval plan + AI Eng handoff if Adopt/Trial
